channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Subnautica 2 CEO Ted Gill Must Be Reinstated, Judge Rules
item.content:encoded:
In a new development in the Subnautica 2 legal saga between the co-founders of developer Unknown Worlds and publisher Krafton, a judge has ruled that former CEO Ted Gill must be reinstated after being fired "without valid cause".

As reported by Kotaku, the judge wrote in a resolution today that Krafton breached its Equity Purchase Agreement (EPA) with Unknown Worlds by firing Gill "and by improperly seizing operational control of Unknown Worlds".

Because of this ruling, the resolution that saw Gill and his fellow co-founders of Unknown Worlds fired is now "ineffective to the extent it infringes on Gill's operational control right", with the judge subsequently ordering Krafton not to get in the way of Gill's "authority" over Subnautica 2's Early Access launch.

Additionally, the terms of a $250 million payout for employees upon reaching a certain revenue goal (which Subnautica 2 would likely have engendered) has been extended; Unknown Worlds is now eligible for said bonus until mid-September, and the date could be extended beyond that as well.

This saga began when Subnautica 2 was delayed beyond its original Early Access window of 2025, with publisher Krafton booting the co-founders of Unknown Worlds out just before the aforementioned payout was due to be shared among the development team. Suspicious timing, no?

Naturally, Gill, along with his fellow co-founders Charlie Cleveland and Max McGuire, subsequently sued Krafton over their dismissal, claiming that Subnautica 2 was ready for release in 2025 and that Krafton intentionally pushed it back to avoid making the $250 million payout.

The terms of that lawsuit were made public following the three co-founders' announcement, and it was reported at the time that Krafton would extend the bonus period for Unknown Worlds employees to cover 2026, which they've now effectively been forced to do by the judge in the lawsuit.

Subnautica 2 is due to land on PC and Xbox Series X|S via Early Access sometime this year, although it's not clear how, or whether, Gill's reinstatement will affect this window. Stay tuned for more.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/subnautica-2-ted-gill-reinstated-lawsuit
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PS5 Pro PSSR Upgrade Rolls Out Across Many Games Starting Today
item.content:encoded:
Sony has revealed that a big PSSR upgrade targeting many major games on the PS5 Pro is launching today, so you can look forward to improved visuals in the likes of Final Fantasy VII Rebirth, Silent Hill f, and more.

As announced in a PlayStation Blog post, the PSSR upgrade, which will only be available on PS5 Pro (as is the feature itself), will deliver "enhanced image stability, improved clarity in fine details, and more consistent performance across supported titles".

Silent Hill f's production team says the upgrade allows them to render grass and shadows with "greater clarity", while BioWare's Maciej Kurowski says Dragon Age: The Veilguard has received a "meaningful improvement in image quality" when using PSSR on PS5 Pro.

A number of other publishers, including Remedy (Alan Wake 2, Control), Ninja Theory (Senua's Saga: Hellblade II), and Square Enix (Final Fantasy VII Rebirth) are taking advantage of the PSSR upgrade in their games, and more will apparently follow.

Despite CD Projekt Red previously saying it had no plans for a PS5 Pro patch for Cyberpunk 2077, that game is also getting a PSSR upgrade in the coming weeks, so that's a nice surprise.

These changes are, it must be said, the kind of differences you probably won't notice if you're not particularly technically-inclined; if you just want your game to look OK and run well, then you probably aren't going to spot what's new.

On the other hand, PS5 Pro owners are likely those who do care about this kind of thing, and if you are looking out for sharper technical details as a result of buying Sony's beefed-up console, then this update should deliver.

In any case, the update will begin rolling out at 10pm Pacific time (that's 1am Eastern on March 17th), and it'll be rolling out "in phases", so it might be a couple of days before you get it in your region. Stay tuned for more.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
